entries.title: House Republican leaders on Friday rejected a Senate-passed bill that would fund most of the Department of Homeland Security
Senators passed funding for most of Homeland Security, moving to end the TSA staffing shortage. They excluded funding for immigration enforcement
If there is one constant in Trump’s Washington, it’s that his deadlines—including about the Iran war—are never really deadlines. Read more in today’s WSJ Politics Newsletter:
Missouri Republican Rep. Sam Graves, chairman of the House Committee on Transportation, is pulling his plans to seek re-election
President Trump said he would sign an executive order to free up money to pay TSA workers, moving to break a deadlock in Congress
Billions of dollars of sophisticated military equipment has been lost or significantly damaged since the U.S. and Israel began striking thousands of targets across Iran more than three weeks ago

A U.S. federal judge on Thursday halted the Trump administration’s designation of Anthropic as a supply-chain risk, issuing a ruling that the government trampled free-speech protections when it classified the company as a security threat
The Trump administration is putting President Trump’s signature on new U.S. paper currency in a first for a sitting president
The Trump administration plans to require higher wages for skilled foreign workers on H-1B visas, its latest move to discourage U.S. companies from hiring foreign nationals.
Lawmakers accused each other Wednesday of being too politically timid to strike a deal to end the standoff over U.S. immigration enforcement
Healthy food advocate Dr. Casey Means, RFK Jr.’s choice for surgeon general, currently lacks enough Republican support to advance her nomination
President Trump has told associates in recent days that he wants to avoid a protracted war in Iran and that he hopes to bring the conflict to an end in the coming weeks
Democrat Emily Gregory beat a Trump-backed Republican in a special election for the Florida state house district that includes President Trump’s Mar-a-Lago estate
The Supreme Court seems inclined to block states from counting mailed ballots that are postmarked by Election Day but arrive a few days afterward
President Trump has demanded that the GOP-controlled Senate vote to end most mail-in voting. Here’s why some Republicans are pushing back
Takeaways from the sprint of March primaries:
The Defense Department introduced new restrictions on press access on Monday, days after a federal judge ruled that key elements of the limits it introduced last year were unconstitutional
Anduril Industries and Palantir Technologies are among firms developing the software to run the $185 billion Golden Dome antimissile shield
The U.S. war with Iran has triggered the broadest and fastest wave of attacks against U.S. embassies and consulates in the post-9/11 era, current and former officials said
Sen. Markwayne Mullin, President Trump’s pick to lead Homeland Security, faced pointed questions from senators on his vision for the department, past comments and previously undisclosed travel
The resignation of Joe Kent, the Trump administration’s top counterterrorism official, has the president’s advisers circling the wagons as signs of dissent over the war emerge
